query: recent studies on PRL3-zumab inhibition of PRL3+ cancer cells in vitro and in vivo

retrieved_docs.title: Zafirlukast -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: A recent study indicates that TMEM16A, a membrane ion channel, is a potential drug target for treating lung adenocarcinoma. Zafirlukast can target the TMEM16A channel to inhibit the proliferation and migration of cells associated with lung adenocarcinoma. Furthermore, in vivo experiments have demonstrated that zafirlukast effectively inhibits the growth of lung adenocarcinoma tumors in mice. The research identified zafirlukast as a novel TMEM16A channel inhibitor with remarkable anticancer properties. Zafirlukast is a promising pharmaceutical candidate for forthcoming preclinical and clinical investigations in individuals with lung adenocarcinoma. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Adrenocortical Cancer -- Medical Oncology -- Targeted Therapy Approach
retrieved_docs.content: Recently, drugs targeting IGF-II seemed to be very promising, as IGF-II is the most-up regulated gene in ACC (80% as described before). Preclinical studies antagonizing this pathway with pharmacological agents resulted in inhibition of growth in vitro and in vivo. This inhibition was more potent than that observed with the use of mitotane alone in decreasing xenograft growth, and the combination of IGF inhibition with mitotane resulted in greater antiproliferative effects than those observed with the use of single-agent treatment. These data establish the role of targeted disruption of IGF-1R signaling to obtain a therapeutic advantage when used with mitotane therapy or possibly other chemotherapeutics in ACC patients. [106] Recently, a phase 2 study used an IMCA12 (cixutumumab), a fully humanized IGF-IR antibody showed a lack of efficacy in a cohort of 19 patients. [107] Also, results of a huge phase 3 trial “GALACCTIC” with a highly specific IGF-IR inhibitor linstinib (OSI-906)in a cohort of 138 metastatic ACC have been recently published: the progression-free and overall survival did not differ between the linstinib and placebo groups. [108] However, in another study, 26 heavily pretreated ACC patients received cixutumumab with temsirolimus, an inhibitor of mammalian targets of IGF-1R signaling (mTOR inhibitor)weekly with restaging at eight weeks. Of these 26 patients, 11 (42%) had stable disease for greater than six months. [109] Finally, because of disease heterogeneity, it appears that using one single agent is not sufficient to induce an objective response. Trials with new targeted substances are underway.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Enkephalin -- Clinical Significance -- Immunoregulation and Cancer Therapy
retrieved_docs.content: Shortly after the experimental discovery of enkephalins, evidence for opioid receptors on the surface of various classes of immune cells began to come to light. [30] [31] By the late 1980s, researchers published direct experimental evidence of antitumor activity utilizing the met-enkephalin/opioid growth factor in the literature. [32] Subsequently, a large body of experimental evidence has detailed the specific effects of MENK/OGF on various types of immune cells. A large body of both in vivo and in vitro studies demonstrates MENK/OGF's ability to slow tumor growth in various cancer types. [16] The mechanism of action for this effect appears to take place through the upregulation of p16 and p21 cyclin-dependent kinases, which stall the progression of the cell replication cycle from the G0/G1 phase to the S phase. Since it is believed that the OGF/OGFr system is tonically active in most, if not all, replicating cell types, it follows that increased concentrations should have an inhibitory effect on replicating cells that are still expressing the OGF receptor and have intact signaling pathways to halt cell progression. MENK/OGF's broad antitumor activity on various cancer types and its relatively benign side effect profile make it a promising adjuvant therapy in cancer patients.

retrieved_docs.title: Pharmaceutics -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The discovery phase of the pharmaceutics process involves establishing a receptor, enzyme, protein, RNA, DNA, or protein that modulates a biochemical process as a potential contributor to the pathophysiology of a disease process. They often base this on literature reviews of basic science investigating disease mechanisms. For example, the immune regulating receptor PD-1 and its ligand PD-L1 were implicated in reducing the immune system's response to cancer. [3] The next stage in the development of PD-1 therapy was then to develop monoclonal antibodies targeted against an area of those proteins that would assure good binding and inhibition of effect. They established in-vitro and in-vivo models to show antibody binding and reactivation of the immune system towards a cancer model. A similar process occurs for traditional small molecules but usually with a screening of activity against a target (typically a receptor or enzyme) against a library of compounds. Enzyme inhibitors such as kinase inhibitors against  BCR-ABL1 in chronic myelogenous leukemia (CML) and acute lymphoblastic leukemia with the Philadelphia chromosome are both examples. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Milk Thistle -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Early lab studies suggest that silymarin and other active molecules in milk thistle may exhibit anti-cancer effects, including interfering with cancer cell division and reproduction, shortening cancer cell lifespan, and reducing the blood supply to tumors. Other research suggests milk thistle acts synergistically with chemotherapy. More research is necessary to determine whether milk thistle can exhibit this activity in vivo. [13] [14] [15] [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Deucravacitinib -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: The drug demonstrates a strong affinity for this domain, making it highly selective for Tyk2 compared to other tyrosine kinases. During in vitro studies, deucravacitinib manifested a 100- to 2000-fold selectivity for Tyk2 compared to Jak1, 2, and 3. Additionally, deucravacitinib exhibited minimal or no binding of Jak1, 2, and 3.

retrieved_docs.title: Teprotumumab -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: The rationale for choosing teprotumumab for the treatment trials in thyroid eye disease was based entirely on in-vitro studies of IGF-1R expressing orbital fibroblast and CD34+ fibrocytes and their critical contribution toward pathophysiology of thyroid orbitopathy, including upregulation of pro-inflammatory cytokine expression, T cell chemoattractant production including IL-16 and RANTES, hyaluronic acid synthesis and enhanced adipogenesis. [21] [22] [23]

retrieved_docs.title: Pharmaceutics -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Preclinical testing, pharmacology, and toxicology testing involve using models of the disease tested. These animal models often harbor close approximations of human diseases, such as human tumor implants in immune-incompetent mice (xenograft tumor models), or are a relatively poor approximation of human pathologies as the forced swimming test. Standard pharmacology models involving testing for cardiovascular, CNS, and renal effects are performed in in-vitro and in-vivo models such as I(Kr) potassium channel antagonism, and prolongation of the QT interval is assessed in telemetered dogs. These are often not required for targeted drugs such as antibodies. Toxicology testing involves standard protocols with doses and duration that extend beyond the expected human dosing (in animal lifetimes). These are often used to determine the first dose used in human trials and determine any particular toxicity concerns that will require monitoring in the early clinical trials.

retrieved_docs.title: Barrett Metaplasia (Archived) -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Aspirin and other NSAIDs have been proposed as chemoprotective agents against adenocarcinoma development in patients with Barrett's esophagus. This data is based on in-vitro and animals based studies. Epidemiological studies have also shown a decreased risk of esophageal adenocarcinoma with NSAID use. Serious gastrointestinal (GI) and cardiovascular side effects associated with NSAIDs may outweigh potential benefits. Therefore, routine use of these drugs is not recommended.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Lipopolysaccharide -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Clinical Research : In clinical research, LPS preparations are useful for elucidating LPS biosynthesis, metabolism, immunology, physiology, and toxicity. LPS is also used to induce the synthesis and secretion of growth-promoting factors such as interleukins in in-vitro and in vivo research studies. Because of its direct function in causing septicemia, LPS has been studied to identify possible antibody targets and inhibitors.

retrieved_docs.title: Dalteparin -- Toxicity -- Carcinogenic Effects
retrieved_docs.content: According to the results of long-term animal studies, dalteparin sodium has unknown potential to cause cancer. However, the medication is non-mutagenic in multiple tests, including the in vitro Ames Test, mouse lymphoma cell forward mutation test, human lymphocyte chromosomal aberration test, and the in vivo mouse micronucleus test. Furthermore, subcutaneous doses of dalteparin sodium up to 1200 IU/kg did not affect male and female fertility or reproductive performance.

retrieved_docs.title: Deucravacitinib -- Administration -- Specific Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Breastfeeding considerations: Data on the secretion of deucravacitinib in human milk, its effects on lactogenesis, and its effects on breastfed infants are not available. During animal studies, deucravacitinib was found in the milk of rats. While prescribing deucravacitinib, the health benefits of breastfeeding infants and the clinical need for therapy must be weighed against the potential adverse effects on the breastfed child. [3] Pediatric patients: The safety and effectiveness of deucravacitinib have not been established in the pediatric patient population.

retrieved_docs.title: Federal Medication Development Regulation -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: The FDA approval process requires comprehensive pre-clinical testing followed by multi-phase clinical trials to ensure safety and efficacy. Discovery is the beginning of medication development involving disease study, molecular models, and high-throughput screening to determine potential new drug molecules. Pre-clinical testing involves in vitro and in vivo experiments using cellular and animal models.

retrieved_docs.title: Nonseminomatous Testicular Tumors -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials
retrieved_docs.content: Researchers recently initiated a study; A Prospective Phase II Trial of Cabazitaxel in Male Patients With Chemotherapy Pre-treated Metastatic Non-seminomatous Germ-cell Tumors. Cabazitaxel is a new generation taxane with a high capacity for blood-brain barrier crossing and limited peripheral neurotoxicity, two major potential advantages in patients with advanced NSGCTs. Cabazitaxel has a broader in vitro spectrum of activity than docetaxel. Taxanes have demonstrated activity in pre-treated GCTs and are now part of standard treatment, but cabazitaxel has not yet been tested in patients with NSGCT.

retrieved_docs.title: Prostate Cancer -- Medical Oncology -- Areas of Future Research
retrieved_docs.content: Targeted, individually customized anticancer therapies offer great potential for controlling malignancy and reducing adverse effects by selectively delivering cytotoxic material to malignant cells. One promising approach involves designed ankyrin repeat proteins (DARPs), non–immunoglobulin-based scaffold proteins engineered to deliver cytotoxic material exclusively to prostate cancer cells. Epithelial cell adhesion molecule (EpCAM) is overexpressed in 40% to 60% of prostate cancers and is associated with more rapid tumor growth, higher risk of metastasis, resistance to chemotherapy, and decreased cancer-specific survival. Experimentally in vitro, it was possible to use a specially designed DARP molecule to deliver a Pseudomonas exotoxin A variant into EpCAM-expressing prostate cancer cells. The toxin was rapidly internalized, and normal prostatic cells were left unharmed. [392] Such therapies offer great hope for individualized, effective, and safe targeted treatments in the near future.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Peptide -- Clinical Significance -- Molecular Imaging and Tumor Targeting
retrieved_docs.content: The mechanism of endogenous peptide and specific receptor binding is principally the design for peptides acting as imaging probes and receptor-binding peptides for overexpressed receptors such as in cancer proliferation. [29] These probes may be strategically designed in vitro to mimic endogenous peptides that ultimately act as biomarkers and allow for the detection of a tumor. The implications of this advanced technology apply to the specific identification of tumor growth and even therapeutic purposes. With advancing sciences and molecular peptide chemistry and a better understanding of more effective targeting, these synthetic peptides have the potential to target multiple disease states with high specificity in imaging modalities such as PET/SPECT, optical imaging, and MRI. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 4 -- Community 2955
retrieved_docs.content: Luteinizing Hormone‑releasing Hormone (LHRH) agonists constitute a class of compounds that stimulate the gonadotropin‑releasing hormone receptor to modulate pituitary hormone release, and triptorelin is a specific agent that belongs to this LHRH‑agonist class. Recognizing triptorelin as a member of the LHRH‑agonist group is essential for understanding its mechanism of action and therapeutic applications in hormone‑dependent conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2762
retrieved_docs.content: PD 134308 is classified as a hormone antagonist, indicating that it functions by blocking hormone receptors. Atosiban is identified as a substance with a peptide hormone receptor antagonist mechanism of action and is also categorized as a hormone antagonist. These relationships show that both PD 134308 and atosiban share the functional property of antagonizing hormone receptors, a characteristic that is important for therapeutic approaches aimed at modulating hormone‑mediated physiological processes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1991
retrieved_docs.content: The mechanism of action for merkaptamin is currently described as an unknown cellular or molecular interaction, indicating that the precise cellular or molecular processes by which merkaptamin exerts its effects have not yet been elucidated.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 2 -- Community 425
retrieved_docs.content: the experimental compound PD‑134308 functions simultaneously as a hormonal antagonist and an antitumor agent directed against the target isa, exemplifying dual pharmacologic activity. This integrated framework delineates classifications, qualifying relationships, therapeutic scopes and specific contraindications of key antineoplastic and inhibitory drugs, supporting informed oncologic patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 4 -- Community 2759
retrieved_docs.content: to act as agonists or antagonists, providing therapeutic benefit in breast cancer, osteoporosis, and other hormonal disorders. Collectively, these allowed qualifiers illustrate the breadth of mechanisms of action and clinical relevance that are accommodated within a regulated, coherent chemical‑synthetic and pharmaceutical context, facilitating consistent scientific handling and regulatory oversight of diverse therapeutic agents.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1253
retrieved_docs.content: Anagrelide is a pharmacologic agent whose mechanism of action involves inhibition of phosphodiesterase enzymes; it functions as a phosphodiesterase inhibitor, a property that underlies its therapeutic effects in relevant clinical contexts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 3 -- Community 2099
retrieved_docs.content: Mifepristone is classified within the group of progesterone receptor modulators, sex hormones, and modulators of the genital system, indicating that it acts on hormonal pathways related to reproductive function. In addition, mifepristone functions as a hormone antagonist, meaning it blocks the action of certain endogenous hormones, particularly progesterone, thereby influencing processes that depend on progesterone signaling. This dual characterization highlights mifepristone’s role both as a member of a specific pharmacologic class and as an antagonist of hormonal activity, which is central to its therapeutic applications in medical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1665
retrieved_docs.content: PD 134308 is classified as a hormone antagonist (isa), placing it within the broader category of antagonisté hormonů. Atosiban is also identified as a member of this hormone‑antagonist class (inverse_isa), meaning it functions as a specific agent that blocks hormonal activity. Both PD 134308 and Atosiban therefore share the common pharmacological property of antagonizing hormone receptors, which is important for understanding their mechanisms of action and potential therapeutic uses.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 1479
retrieved_docs.content: Demekolcin is classified as a member of the colchicine‑derivative antineoplastic group, indicating that it shares the structural backbone of colchicine and possesses anticancer activity. As part of this class, demekolcin is expected to exhibit the pharmacologic characteristics typical of colchicine derivatives used in oncology, such as interference with microtubule dynamics to inhibit tumor cell proliferation. Recognizing demekolcin’s membership in the colchicine‑derivative antineoplastic category helps clinicians understand its mechanism of action, potential therapeutic applications, and safety profile within the broader context of antineoplastic agents derived from colchicine.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 2560
retrieved_docs.content: Luteinizing Hormone‑releasing Hormone (LHRH) agonists are classified within the broader category of hormones and related agents, reflecting their role in modulating the endocrine system. Within this class, triptorelin represents a specific member, exemplifying the pharmacologic application of LHRH agonists in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 2457
retrieved_docs.content: PD 134308 is classified as a hormone antagonist. Atosiban is likewise classified as a hormone antagonist and is a synthetic analogue that is derived from the natural peptide vasotocin. Both PD 134308 and atosiban belong to the hormone‑antagonist class, indicating that they act by blocking specific hormonal pathways, and the link between atosiban and vasotocin highlights the latter’s origin as a modified form of the endogenous hormone. This shared classification informs their pharmacological use as agents that inhibit hormone‑mediated effects.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1418
retrieved_docs.content: PITUITARY AND HYPOTHALAMIC HORMONES AND ANALOGUES are classified as hypothalamic hormones, encompassing both the natural hormones secreted by the pituitary and hypothalamus and their synthetic analogues. This categorization places them within the broader group of hypothalamic hormones, highlighting their shared origin and functional relevance in endocrine regulation and therapeutic applications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2020
retrieved_docs.content: Cabozantinib and midostaurin are both classified as protein kinase inhibitors within the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) system under the category “Other protein kinase inhibitors.” This classification indicates that each drug belongs to the same therapeutic group of agents that target protein kinases, reflecting shared mechanisms of action and relevance in the treatment of diseases such as cancer.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2641
retrieved_docs.content: Medroxyprogesteronacetát is classified as a type of antikoncepční látky ženské (female contraceptive agent). In clinical pharmacology, medroxyprogesteronacetát exerts its contraceptive effect by binding to and modulating the human progesterone receptor (Progesterone Receptor, human), which is its primary molecular target. This interaction underlies the hormone‑based mechanism by which medroxyprogesteronacetát prevents ovulation and maintains contraceptive efficacy in women.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 1679
retrieved_docs.content: L‑karnitin and metreleptin are both classified as members of the broader category “amino acids and derivatives,” indicating that each compound is biochemically derived from amino‑acid structures. This grouping reflects their shared origin in amino‑acid metabolism, even though their physiological roles differ: L‑karnitin functions primarily in the transport of long‑chain fatty acids into mitochondria for β‑oxidation, while metreleptin is a recombinant analog of the hormone leptin used therapeutically to replace deficient leptin activity. Recognizing both agents as amino‑acid‑derived molecules highlights the diverse functional spectrum of this chemical class in normal metabolism and in clinical interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 2659
retrieved_docs.content: The substance with peptide hormone receptor antagonist mechanism of action, known as atosiban, functions as an inverse ISA, indicating that it antagonizes peptide hormone receptors while exhibiting inverse agonist properties.
